title: explore packit-service for fedora package maintenance
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

We should explore [Packit-as-a-Service](https://packit.dev/packit-as-a-service/) for Fedora package maintenance and check if it makes everyone happy. If that handles CentOS+OBS maintenance as well, even better.

If we have agreement, I'm thinking maybe @ashley-cui can take the lead on this, and I will help her out with packaging issues or whatever.

comments.body: I can help with the Fedora side of things.
hi @nasirhm , looks like @ashley-cui might be occupied for the next few weeks. Would you be interested in taking this forward?
Sure, I would love to work on it :)
@nasirhm great, it's all yours :) , let me know if you hit any roadblocks. Thanks a lot!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hey @lsm5, Thank you very much for the ping and checking in on me on IRC, I'm actually new to the Packit automation and am exploring it with Packaging podman. 

Sorry for sharing no updates in the past month.

Once, I've the basic configuration ready, I'll be sharing it here. It'll most probably take me a week for it.

Update: I've tried building multiple RPMs for Podman to get my self familiar with How `build_rpm.sh` works, 

To setup packit-service, we would have to make sure that packit builds the RPM without having to run any initial scripts.

and upon typing: `$ packit srpm` there needs to be a valid SPEC file (not a template) for packit to build the source RPM from.

I was wondering, If packit has some feature where we can add some thing like: Run this script before building the RPM for it's configuration file. 

or, How about creating a `Makefile` with a rule: `make packit_srpm` which will first cause the script to run what'll result in a working SPEC file and then running `packit srpm` to build the SRPM from it.

@lsm5 can you please provide some pointers on How can we tackle it (I'm nasirhm on IRC), I'm actively trying some ideas on my Local fork's `packit_configuration` branch, [here](https://github.com/nasirhm/podman/blob/packit_configutation/.packit.yaml).
It should be fairly simple to do this in the Makefile.
I'm on it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@nasirhm Did you make any progress?
@nasirhm thanks a lot. Let us know if any blockers.
@nasirhm also, please let me know whenever you reach the stage where you need to touch files in podman dist-git. I have my own automation running which I'll need to stop. Thanks!
Friendly ping.  Did the project progress?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
No response from reporter, closing, Reopen if you have more data.
I'll take this up hopefully sometime next month. Will reopen then.
@nasirhm I'm taking this up for now. You're always welcome to jump in whenever you're free.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lsm5 friendly ping
I've got some time to work on it, Do we have any progress @lsm5 ?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@nasirhm @lsm5 What is going on with this issue?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Let's close this. We have the `podman.spec.rpkg` which sends builds for every PR to `rhcontainerbot/podman-next` copr. And we'll have a separate cirrus job in the future for fedora rawhide gating tests. I think this would just become additional non-cirrus maintanenace.
